content.thinking:

Let me start by:
1. Reading the progress file to understand current state
2. Reading features.json to see what's implemented
3. Running the app with `bun tauri dev`
4. Testing visually with Playwright MCP

   466→        "UPDATE": "Regenerate embedding when title/description/content changes",
   467→        "DELETE": "Cascade delete embedding when task/progress is deleted"
   468→      },
   469→      "embeddingService": {
   470→        "pattern": "Singleton with lazy initialization",
   471→        "modelLoading": "Load model on first embedding request, reuse thereafter",
   472→        "caching": "Model files cached in ~/.cache/huggingface/"
   473→      },
   474→      "benefits": [
   475→        "'OAuth' finds 'OAuth2' and 'authentication'",
   476→        "'bug fix' finds 'error correction' and 'issue resolved'",
   477→        "Natural language queries work intuitively"
   478→      ],
   479→      "storage": "~1.5KB per embedding (384 floats * 4 bytes)",
   480→      "performance": "First embedding ~3s (model load), subsequent ~50ms"
   481→    },
   482→    "references": [
   483→      "https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec",
   484→      "https://github.com/Anush008/fastembed-rs",
   485→      "https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/rust.html"
   486→    ]
   487→  }
   488→]
   489→

[
  {
    "id": "F001",
    "description": "Multi-project tab management - Open and manage multiple projects via tabs",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create TabBar component with PatternFly Tabs",
      "Implement tab state in @zazcart/tools state-machine",
      "Add new tab (+) and close tab (X) functionality",
      "Support tab reordering via drag-and-drop",
      "Display project name from package.json or folder name"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F002",
    "description": "Kanban board view - Visual feature tracking with BACKLOG|TODO|DOING|REVIEW|QA|DONE columns",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create KanbanBoard component with 6 columns",
      "Create KanbanCard component for feature display",
      "Read features from .harness/features.json",
      "Map feature status to appropriate column",
      "Support card expansion for details"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F003",
    "description": "Real-time log panel - Live output from running processes with ANSI color support",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create LogPanel component with virtual scroll",
      "Implement ANSI color parsing",
      "Add process filter dropdown",
      "Implement search within logs",
      "Add auto-scroll toggle and clear button"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F004",
    "description": "PTY process controller - Spawn and control shell processes via Rust backend",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add portable-pty crate to Cargo.toml",
      "Implement pty_spawn Tauri command",
      "Implement pty_write for sending keystrokes",
      "Implement pty_resize for terminal resize",
      "Implement pty_kill for process termination",
      "Stream PTY output via Tauri events"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F005",
    "description": "Harness state visualization - Display progress.txt and features.json status (Update: display data from DB, maybe using our harness commandline api)",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "medium",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create ProgressCard component",
      "Display features completed/total",
      "Show current session info",
      "Display last commit from git log",
      "Highlight active feature"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F006",
    "description": "Project selector dialog - Open projects from file system",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create ProjectSelector dialog with PatternFly Modal",
      "Implement recent projects list with localStorage",
      "Use Tauri file picker (@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog) for browse functionality",
      "Validate project (check for .harness folder via Rust command)",
      "Store recent projects in local storage with timestamps"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F007",
    "description": "Settings panel - Configure HQ preferences",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "low",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create Settings view",
      "Implement theme toggle (light/dark/system)",
      "Configure log buffer size",
      "Set default shell",
      "Configure PTY settings"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F008",
    "description": "File watcher - Watch .harness/ for changes and auto-update UI (update: we now use DB, rethink)",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add notify crate to Cargo.toml",
      "Implement harness_watch Tauri command",
      "Emit events on features.json changes",
      "Emit events on progress.txt changes",
      "Debounce file change events (500ms)"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F009",
    "description": "State management setup - Configure @zazcart/tools state machine",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create store.ts with createMachine and createSlice",
      "Define types: Project, Feature, Process, LogEntry",
      "Create slices: ui, projects, features, processes, logs",
      "Implement selectors for derived state",
      "Connect to React components (pending @zazcart/tools hook fix)"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F010",
    "description": "Tauri project initialization - Setup Tauri 2.x with React 19",
    "category": "setup",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Run bun create tauri-app with react-ts template",
      "Add React 19 and PatternFly 6 dependencies",
      "Add @zazcart/tools dependency",
      "Configure vite.config.ts",
      "Verify app runs with bun tauri dev"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F011",
    "description": "Dev Bridge - Secure RPC bridge for browser-to-native communication during development/testing",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Research: Document limitations of browser-only mode vs Tauri native",
      "Design: Define secure RPC protocol (WebSocket or HTTP) with authentication",
      "Implement: Create Rust dev server that exposes Tauri commands via RPC",
      "Implement: Create TypeScript client that uses RPC when not in Tauri context",
      "Security: Add token-based auth and localhost-only binding",
      "Testing: Verify Playwright can test PTY and native features via bridge"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "problem": "Tauri invoke() only works in native webview context. Playwright tests run in browser (localhost:5173) which has no access to Rust backend. This blocks testing of PTY, file system, and other native features.",
      "constraints": [
        "Must NOT introduce security vulnerabilities (no arbitrary code execution)",
        "Must only work in development mode (disabled in production builds)",
        "Should be extensible for future plugin SDK without over-engineering now"
      ],
      "approach": "Create a dev-only WebSocket/HTTP server in Rust that mirrors Tauri commands. TypeScript client auto-detects context and routes to Tauri invoke or RPC bridge. Token auth prevents unauthorized access.",
      "future_consideration": "Plugin SDK may use similar RPC pattern for sandboxed plugin communication"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F012",
    "description": "Native UX Polish - Make the app feel like a native macOS application, not a web app",
    "category": "ux",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "estimatedEffort": "9 hours",
    "platform": "macOS only",
    "steps": [
      "Phase 0: Native Menu Bar - Create macOS-style menu (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help) in src-tauri/src/lib.rs using Tauri Menu API",
      "Phase 1: CSS Foundation - Add native-feel styles to src/index.css (font-size 14px, user-select:none, :focus-visible, cursor:default, overscroll-behavior:none)",
      "Phase 2: Disable Browser Behaviors - Add tauri-plugin-prevent-default to block Cmd+F, DevTools (prod), zoom, browser context menu",
      "Phase 3: Window Vibrancy - Add window-vibrancy crate, configure transparent window with blur in tauri.conf.json (macOSPrivateApi, titleBarStyle:Overlay)",
      "Phase 4: Context Menus - Create src/components/ContextMenu.tsx using PatternFly Menu+Popper (Notion-style, not native)",
      "Phase 5: Keybindings - Create VSCode-style configurable shortcuts: src/config/defaultKeybindings.json + src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "Phase 6: Animations - Add motion library, apply spring physics to tabs, cards, panels transitions",
      "Phase 7: Focus/Blur State - Create src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts, dim UI when window loses focus"
    ],
    "phases": {
      "phase0_native_menu": {
        "description": "Native Menu Bar (macOS style)",
        "effort": "1 hour",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "problem": "Current menu looks like web app (HQ, Edit...), not native macOS",
          "solution": "Use Tauri Menu API with PredefinedMenuItem for standard macOS menus",
          "menus": ["HQ (About, Settings, Services, Hide, Quit)", "File (Open Project, New Tab, Close Tab)", "Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All)", "View (Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Logs, Fullscreen)", "Window (Minimize, Maximize, Prev/Next Tab)", "Help (Documentation, GitHub)"]
        }
      },
      "phase1_css_foundation": {
        "description": "CSS Foundation (No dependencies)",
        "effort": "30 min",
        "files": ["src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "font": "ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system at 14px (native apps use 13-14px, not 16px)",
          "userSelect": "none on all UI elements, text only on content (.selectable, pre, code, .log-content)",
          "cursor": "default everywhere, pointer only for external links a[href^='http']",
          "focus": ":focus { outline: none } + :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ... }",
          "drag": "-webkit-user-drag: none on images, -webkit-app-region: drag for titlebar",
          "overscroll": "overscroll-behavior: none to prevent bounce",
          "background": "html, body { background: transparent } for vibrancy"
        }
      },
      "phase2_prevent_default": {
        "description": "Disable Browser Behaviors",
        "effort": "15 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "crate": "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\"",
          "disables": ["Cmd+F (find)", "Cmd+P (print)", "Cmd+G (find next)", "Cmd+[+/-] (zoom)", "Right-click context menu (we use custom)", "F5/Cmd+R reload (production)", "Pinch-to-zoom"],
          "devMode": "Keep DEV_TOOLS and RELOAD enabled in debug mode",
          "usage": "tauri_plugin_prevent_default::Builder::new().with_flags(Flags::all() - Flags::DEV_TOOLS - Flags::RELOAD).build()"
        }
      },
      "phase3_vibrancy": {
        "description": "Window Vibrancy (macOS blur effect)",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "crate": "window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"",
          "tauriConfig": {
            "macOSPrivateApi": true,
            "windows": [{ "transparent": true, "decorations": true, "titleBarStyle": "Overlay", "hiddenTitle": true }]
          },
          "rustCode": "apply_vibrancy(&window, NSVisualEffectMaterial::Sidebar, None, None)",
          "materials": ["Sidebar (recommended)", "HudWindow (darker)", "FullScreenUI", "ContentBackground"],
          "limitation": "macOS only, Windows/Linux will have solid background"
        }
      },
      "phase4_context_menus": {
        "description": "Custom Context Menus (Notion-style using PatternFly)",
        "effort": "1.5 hours",
        "files": ["src/components/ContextMenu.tsx", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "approach": "Use PatternFly Menu + Popper (already installed) instead of native Tauri menus for full styling control",
          "components": ["Menu", "MenuList", "MenuItem", "MenuGroup", "Divider"],
          "features": ["Position at click coordinates", "Escape to close", "Click outside to close", "Keyboard navigation (built-in PatternFly)", "Shortcut display (⌘C, ⌘V)", "Danger items (red)"],
          "inspiration": "Notion, Linear - custom rendered menus, not OS native"
        }
      },
      "phase5_keybindings": {
        "description": "Configurable Keybindings (VSCode-style)",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["src/config/defaultKeybindings.json", "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts", "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts"],
        "details": {
          "format": "VSCode keybindings.json: { key, command, when?, args? }",
          "defaultShortcuts": {
            "cmd+t": "tab.new",
            "cmd+w": "tab.close",
            "cmd+shift+[": "tab.previous",
            "cmd+shift+]": "tab.next",
            "cmd+,": "settings.open",
            "cmd+k": "commandPalette.open",
            "cmd+\\": "sidebar.toggle",
            "cmd+j": "logs.toggle",
            "cmd+o": "project.open",
            "escape": "modal.close (when: modalOpen)"
          },
          "userConfig": "~/.config/hq/keybindings.json for user overrides",
          "features": ["Command registry pattern", "When clause for context-aware shortcuts", "Hot-reload on file change", "Merge user overrides with defaults"]
        }
      },
      "phase6_animations": {
        "description": "Spring Animations",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["package.json", "src/components/*.tsx"],
        "details": {
          "library": "motion (formerly Framer Motion)",
          "install": "bun add motion",
          "animations": {
            "tabSwitch": "spring with stiffness: 400, damping: 30",
            "cardExpand": "type: spring, bounce: 0.2",
            "panelResize": "type: tween, ease: easeOut",
            "fadeIn": "initial: { opacity: 0, y: 10 }, animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }"
          },
          "why": "Physical spring physics feel native, not 'webby' easing curves"
        }
      },
      "phase7_focus_blur": {
        "description": "Focus/Blur Window State",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs", "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "rustEvent": "window.on_window_event for Focused(bool), emit 'window-focus' to frontend",
          "frontendHook": "useWindowFocus() returns isFocused, adds .window-unfocused class to html",
          "cssEffect": "Dim colors when unfocused: .window-unfocused .tab-active { background: gray }",
          "inspiration": "Native macOS apps dim selection highlight when window loses focus"
        }
      }
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "rust": ["window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"", "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\""],
      "npm": ["motion"]
    },
    "filesToCreate": [
      "src/config/defaultKeybindings.json",
      "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts",
      "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts",
      "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx"
    ],
    "filesToModify": [
      "src-tauri/Cargo.toml",
      "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json",
      "src-tauri/src/lib.rs",
      "src/index.css",
      "package.json"
    ],
    "successCriteria": [
      "Menu bar looks native macOS (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help)",
      "No focus outlines on mouse click, only on Tab navigation",
      "Text not selectable on buttons, labels, headers",
      "Cmd+F, Cmd+P, right-click 'Inspect' disabled in production",
      "DevTools accessible in dev mode only",
      "Window has translucent blur effect (macOS vibrancy)",
      "Right-click shows custom PatternFly context menu (Notion-style)",
      "Keyboard shortcuts configurable via JSON file",
      "Cmd+T/W/[/] work for tab management",
      "UI dims when window loses focus",
      "Animations feel 'springy', not 'tweeny'"
    ],
    "references": [
      "https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/keybindings",
      "https://www.patternfly.org/components/menus/menu",
      "https://github.com/tauri-apps/window-vibrancy",
      "https://github.com/ferreira-tb/tauri-plugin-prevent-default",
      "https://getlotus.app/21-making-electron-apps-feel-native-on-mac",
      "https://motion.dev/"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "platform": "macOS only for this implementation",
      "inspiration": ["Linear", "Notion", "Warp", "Arc", "Raycast", "VSCode"],
      "knownLimitations": [
        "Vibrancy is macOS-only",
        "backdrop-filter may conflict with window transparency (Tauri 2.2.5 bug)",
        "DevTools kept in dev mode for debugging"
      ]
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F013",
    "description": "Storage Architecture - SQLite + FTS5 database for projects managed by HQ",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add Rust dependencies (rusqlite, ulid, chrono)",
      "Create db/types.rs - TaskStatus enum, structs with composite ID helpers",
      "Create db/schema.rs - CREATE TABLE SQL with FTS5 and triggers",
      "Create db/connection.rs - DB open, pragmas, schema initialization",
      "Create db/tasks.rs - CRUD + FTS search for project_tasks",
      "Create db/steps.rs - CRUD for task_steps with composite IDs",
      "Create db/sessions.rs - CRUD for work_sessions",
      "Create db/progress.rs - CRUD + FTS search for project_progress",
      "Create db/pagination.rs - Cursor-based pagination helpers",
      "Create db/migrate.rs - Legacy TXT/JSON to SQLite migration",
      "Register db module in lib.rs with Tauri commands"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "idStrategy": "ULID for standalone entities, composite IDs (parent#ulid#child_ulid) for children",
      "fts5": "External content tables synced via triggers",
      "pagination": "Cursor-based with prev/next only"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F014",
    "description": "CLI Utility - harness command for AI agents and humans to interact with storage",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add clap dependency for argument parsing",
      "Create bin/harness.rs - CLI entry point with subcommands",
      "Implement 'harness init' - create .harness/harness.db",
      "Implement 'harness task' subcommands (add, list, search, get, update, delete)",
      "Implement 'harness step' subcommands (add, complete, list, delete)",
      "Implement 'harness session' subcommands (start, end, list, current)",
      "Implement 'harness progress' subcommands (add, list, search)",
      "Implement 'harness migrate' - import from legacy TXT/JSON",
      "Implement 'harness status' - project summary",
      "Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "outputFormats": "--json for AI, --quiet for IDs only, table by default",
      "exitCodes": "0=success, 1=error, 2=not harness project, 3=db error"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F015",
    "description": "Visual Test Screenshots - Store and view Playwright test screenshots in HQ app",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add screenshots table to schema (id, task_id or step_id, file_path, created_at)",
      "Store screenshots in .harness/screenshots/ with naming: {task_id}_{timestamp}.png",
      "Add harness screenshot add <task_id> <file_path> CLI command",
      "Add harness screenshot list [--task=X] CLI command",
      "Create ScreenshotViewer component in frontend",
      "Display screenshots in task detail view",
      "Add thumbnail gallery for task with multiple screenshots",
      "Support image zoom/lightbox view"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "naming": "Screenshots named with task/step ID reference for traceability",
      "storage": "Files in .harness/screenshots/, metadata in SQLite",
      "requirement": "All frontend tasks must have visual tests with screenshots"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F016",
    "description": "Semantic Search - Vector embeddings with sqlite-vec + fastembed for intelligent search",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add Rust dependencies (sqlite-vec, fastembed, zerocopy)",
      "Create db/embeddings.rs - EmbeddingService singleton with lazy model init",
      "Add task_embeddings vec0 table to schema (rowid, embedding float[384])",
      "Add progress_embeddings vec0 table to schema (rowid, embedding float[384])",
      "Modify create_task() - auto-generate embedding on INSERT",
      "Modify update_task() - regenerate embedding when title/description changes",
      "Modify delete_task() - cascade delete embedding",
      "Modify add_progress() - auto-generate embedding on INSERT",
      "Modify update_progress_content() - regenerate embedding on UPDATE",
      "Modify delete_progress() - cascade delete embedding",
      "Implement search_tasks_semantic() - KNN search returning ranked results",
      "Implement search_progress_semantic() - KNN search for progress",
      "Add harness task search --semantic 'query' CLI command",
      "Add harness progress search --semantic 'query' CLI command",
      "Add harness embeddings rebuild CLI command (reindex all)",
      "Add harness embeddings status CLI command (stats)"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "model": "AllMiniLML6V2 (384 dimensions, ~30MB, fast)",
      "libraries": {
        "sqlite-vec": "0.1.7-alpha.2 - Vector search SQLite extension",
        "fastembed": "Latest - Local ONNX embeddings",
        "zerocopy": "For efficient vector byte conversion"
      },
      "crudSync": {
        "CREATE": "Auto-generate embedding when task/progress is created",
        "UPDATE": "Regenerate embedding when title/description/content changes",
        "DELETE": "Cascade delete embedding when task/progress is deleted"
      },
      "embeddingService": {
        "pattern": "Singleton with lazy initialization",
        "modelLoading": "Load model on first embedding request, reuse thereafter",
        "caching": "Model files cached in ~/.cache/huggingface/"
      },
      "benefits": [
        "'OAuth' finds 'OAuth2' and 'authentication'",
        "'bug fix' finds 'error correction' and 'issue resolved'",
        "Natural language queries work intuitively"
      ],
      "storage": "~1.5KB per embedding (384 floats * 4 bytes)",
      "performance": "First embedding ~3s (model load), subsequent ~50ms"
    },
    "references": [
      "https://github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec",
      "https://github.com/Anush008/fastembed-rs",
      "https://alexgarcia.xyz/sqlite-vec/rust.html"
    ]
  }
]

1→# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
     2→# Session Progress Log
     3→
     4→================================================================================
     5→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
     6→================================================================================
     7→
     8→## What was done:
     9→- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
    10→- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
    11→- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
    12→- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
    13→- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
    14→- Installed dependencies:
    15→  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
    16→  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
    17→  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
    18→  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
    19→  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
    20→  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
    21→- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
    22→- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup
    23→
    24→## Key decisions:
    25→- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
    26→- State management: @zazcart/tools
    27→- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
    28→- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)
    29→
    30→## Next steps:
    31→- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
    32→- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
    33→- F001: Implement multi-project tab management
    34→
    35→## Notes:
    36→- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
    37→- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components
    38→
    39→================================================================================
    40→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
    41→================================================================================
    42→
    43→## What was done:
    44→- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
    45→  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
    46→  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
    47→  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
    48→- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
    49→- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
    50→- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
    51→- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP
    52→

   240→================================================================================
   241→TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
   242→================================================================================
   243→
   244→## Problem Statement
   245→
   246→Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
   247→(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).
   248→
   249→When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
   250→which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:
   251→
   252→### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
   253→- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
   254→- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
   255→- File system operations (read/write project files)
   256→- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
   257→- System notifications
   258→- Any Rust backend functionality
   259→
   260→### What DOES work in browser mode:
   261→- React components and UI rendering
   262→- State management (@zazcart/tools)
   263→- Mock data and simulated interactions
   264→- PatternFly components
   265→- Pure frontend logic
   266→
   267→## Why This Matters
   268→
   269→1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
   270→2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
   271→3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality
   272→
   273→## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge
   274→
   275→Create a development-only RPC bridge that:
   276→
   277→```
   278→┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
   279→│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
   280→│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
   281→│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
   282→│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
   283→│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
   284→└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
   285→```
   286→
   287→### Architecture
   288→
   289→1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   290→   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   291→   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   292→   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   293→   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)
   294→
   295→2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   296→   ```typescript
   297→   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   298→   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
   299→     if (isTauri()) {
   300→       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
   301→     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
   302→       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
   303→     } else {
   304→       throw new Error('No backend available');
   305→     }
   306→   }
   307→   ```
   308→
   309→3. **Security Measures**:
   310→   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   311→   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   312→   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   313→   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   314→   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)
   315→
   316→### Future SDK Consideration
   317→
   318→The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
   319→- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
   320→- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
   321→- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
   322→- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer
   323→
   324→**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
   325→design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.
   326→
   327→## Implementation Steps
   328→
   329→See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly 6 renamed Text/TextContent to Content
- Page component uses masthead prop, not header
- Label colors: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orangered, grey, yellow

## F010: PASSED ✅
- Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 running
- Icons generated from SVG source

## Next steps:
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F009 Complete - State Management Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created src/state/store.ts with @zazcart/tools
- Defined 5 slices:
  - ui: activeTab, theme, projectOrder, sidebarCollapsed, logPanelHeight
  - projects: Record<string, Project>
  - processes: Record<string, Process>
  - logs: entries with ring buffer (max 10000), autoScroll, filter
  - features: items[], loading, error
- Defined TypeScript interfaces: Project, Feature, Process, LogEntry
- Created selectors: activeProject, projectsArray, activeProcesses, logsForProcess, featureStats
- Created src/state/index.ts for exports
- Updated App.tsx to display state info

## Key learnings:
- @zazcart/tools useMachine hook causes infinite loop with getSnapshot
- Added @ts-nocheck temporarily due to type inference issues
- Using local useState as fallback until hook issues resolved

## F009: PASSED ✅
- State machine configured with 5 slices
- Types and selectors ready
- Visual test passed

## Next steps:
- F002: Implement Kanban board view

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F001 Complete - Multi-project Tab Management
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created TabBar component (src/components/TabBar.tsx)
- Implemented tab functionality:
  - Add new project tabs via (+) button
  - Close tabs via (X) button (only visible when >1 tab)
  - Select tabs to switch between projects
  - Drag-and-drop prepared (console.log for now)
- Updated App.tsx to integrate TabBar with mock projects
- Fixed PatternFly button nesting issue (span with role="button" instead)
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly Tab is already a button, cannot nest Button inside
- Use span with role="button" for accessible close button inside tabs
- isOverflowHorizontal prop handles tab overflow automatically

## F001: PASSED ✅
- TabBar renders project tabs
- Add/Close/Select functionality works
- Console clean (no errors)

## Next steps:
- F003: Implement real-time log panel

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F002 Complete - Kanban Board View
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created KanbanBoard component (src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx)
  - 6 columns: BACKLOG, TODO, DOING, REVIEW, QA, DONE
  - Badge with feature count per column
  - Features sorted by priority within columns
  - Horizontal scroll for responsive layout
- Created KanbanCard component (src/components/KanbanCard.tsx)
  - Expandable card with description and steps
  - Labels for priority (critical/high/medium/low)
  - Labels for category (core/ui/setup)
  - Visual icons for step completion status

TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
================================================================================

## Problem Statement

Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).

When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:

### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
- File system operations (read/write project files)
- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
- System notifications
- Any Rust backend functionality

### What DOES work in browser mode:
- React components and UI rendering
- State management (@zazcart/tools)
- Mock data and simulated interactions
- PatternFly components
- Pure frontend logic

## Why This Matters

1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality

## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge

Create a development-only RPC bridge that:

```
┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
```

### Architecture

1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)

2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   ```typescript
   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
     if (isTauri()) {
       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
     } else {
       throw new Error('No backend available');
     }
   }
   ```

3. **Security Measures**:
   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)

### Future SDK Consideration

The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer

**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.

## Implementation Steps

See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

## What was done:
- Implemented F013: SQLite Storage Architecture
  - Added Rust dependencies: rusqlite 0.38 (bundled), ulid 1.2, chrono 0.4, thiserror 2.0, base64 0.22
  - Created db/types.rs: TaskStatus enum, ProjectTask, TaskStep, WorkSession, ProjectProgress
  - Created db/schema.rs: SQLite + FTS5 schema with triggers
  - Created db/connection.rs: Connection management, DbStats
  - Created db/tasks.rs: Task CRUD, pagination, FTS5 search
  - Created db/steps.rs: Step CRUD with composite IDs
  - Created db/sessions.rs: Session lifecycle management
  - Created db/progress.rs: Progress CRUD with FTS5 search
  - Total: 133 unit tests passing

- Implemented F014: CLI Utility (`harness` binary)
  - Added clap 4.5 for command parsing
  - Created bin/harness.rs CLI with commands:
    - `harness init`: Create .harness/harness.db
    - `harness db status`: Show DB info and stats
    - `harness task add/list/search/get/update/delete/reorder`
    - `harness step add/list/complete/uncomplete/toggle/delete`
    - `harness session start/end/current/list/get`
    - `harness progress add/list/search`
    - `harness status`: Project summary
  - Three output formats: text (default), json, quiet (IDs only)
  - Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=not harness project, 3=db error

## Key design decisions:
- ULID for standalone IDs (26 chars, sortable by time)
- Composite IDs: `{parent_table}#{parent_ulid}#{child_ulid}`
- ISO 8601 dates, TEXT booleans ('true'/'false'), UPPERCASE enums
- FTS5 external content tables with sync triggers
- Cursor-based pagination (prev/next cursors)
- WAL mode for concurrent access

## Architecture:
```
project/.harness/harness.db
├── project_tasks (ULID pk, FTS5 search)
├── task_steps (composite ID: task#{ulid}#{ulid})
├── work_sessions (ULID pk)
├── project_progress (composite ID: session#{ulid}#{ulid}, FTS5 search)
└── schema_metadata (version tracking)
```

- 133 unit tests covering all CRUD operations
- Integration test: harness init creates valid database

  - task_id (optional FK to project_tasks)
  - step_id (optional reference)
  - file_name (relative to .harness/screenshots/)
  - caption (optional description)
  - created_at (ISO 8601 timestamp)
  - Indexes: task_id, created_at

### Rust Backend:
- Added TestScreenshot type to db/types.rs with builder pattern
- Created db/screenshots.rs module with CRUD operations:
  - create_screenshot / add_screenshot
  - get_screenshot
  - list_screenshots / list_screenshots_for_task
  - count_screenshots / count_screenshots_for_task
  - update_screenshot_caption / update_screenshot_task
  - delete_screenshot / delete_screenshots_for_task
- Exported module in db/mod.rs
- 13 unit tests: ALL PASSING

### CLI Commands:
- Added screenshot subcommand to harness CLI:
  - harness screenshot add <file> [-t task_id] [-c caption]
  - harness screenshot list [-t task_id] [-l limit]
  - harness screenshot get <id>
  - harness screenshot delete <id>
  - harness screenshot stats
- All three output formats: text, json, quiet

### Frontend:
- Created ScreenshotViewer component (src/components/ScreenshotViewer.tsx):

  - Modal-based lightbox for viewing
  - Empty state for no screenshots
  - Loading state with spinner
  - Motion animations for smooth transitions
  - Task-filtered and global views
- Exported in components/index.ts

- File storage: .harness/screenshots/{file_name}
- Metadata in SQLite, actual files on disk

content.thinking:
Agora entendo. O RPC client (`src/lib/rpc.ts`) exporta:
- `invoke<T>(cmd, args)` - função unificada que funciona em Tauri e browser
- `listen<T>(event, handler)` - para escutar eventos
- `isTauri()` - para verificar contexto

Os arquivos que precisam ser corrigidos:
1. `ProgressCard.tsx` - linha 28: `import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core'`
2. `ScreenshotViewer.tsx` - linha 22: `import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core'`
3. `ProjectSelector.tsx` - linha 32: `import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core'`
4. `useHarnessWatcher.ts` - linhas 10-11: imports do `@tauri-apps/api`

Preciso trocar para usar o `invoke` e `listen` do `src/lib/rpc.ts`.

Há dois problemas:

Vou verificar o App.tsx primeiro.

No App.tsx, o código usa:
```javascript
listen('menu:open_project', () => { ... })
```

Isso deve funcionar porque o callback não usa o payload.

content.thinking:
Ainda vejo o erro "Dev bridge not available" mesmo depois de conectar. O problema é que a primeira chamada acontece antes da conexão completar. A race condition ainda persiste.

Mas a UI está funcionando bem no geral - a aplicação carrega com todos os componentes (Kanban, Logs, Terminal, etc). Os erros são apenas para o ProgressCard tentando fazer chamadas RPC que falham por timing.

Para o teste de happy path, a UI está renderizando corretamente:
- Header com "HQ - Harness Orchestrator"
- TabBar funcionando
- KanbanBoard com 6 colunas
- LogPanel com logs de mock
- TerminalPanel em modo Dev Bridge

Vou esperar um pouco e verificar se a conexão estabiliza após algumas chamadas de retry.

5. ✅ TerminalPanel em modo Dev Bridge

This is a timing issue - the ProgressCard and useHarnessWatcher are trying to invoke commands before the Dev Bridge WebSocket connection is fully established. However, the PTY Terminal did work (we saw the shell output), which means the Dev Bridge is functional once connected.

content.text:
## Resumo do Teste Happy Path

### ✅ Componentes Funcionando

| Componente | Status | Observação |
|------------|--------|------------|
| **TabBar** | ✅ OK | Tabs renderizam, navegação funciona |
| **KanbanBoard** | ✅ OK | 6 colunas, cards com features |
| **LogPanel** | ✅ OK | Logs mock com ANSI colors, filtros |
| **TerminalPanel** | ✅ OK | PTY spawna via Dev Bridge, output streamed |
| **Settings** | ✅ OK | Theme, Logs, PTY configs funcionam |
| **ProgressCard** | ⚠️ Parcial | UI renderiza, mas harness commands falham |
| **ProjectSelector** | ✅ OK | Dialog abre corretamente |

### ⚠️ Problema Identificado: Race Condition no Dev Bridge

Os erros no console mostram que componentes tentam invocar comandos **antes** da conexão WebSocket estar estabelecida:

```
Failed to run harness command: Dev bridge not available
```

**Causa:** `ProgressCard` e `useHarnessWatcher` chamam `invoke()` durante mount, mas o WebSocket ainda está em `CONNECTING`.

**Impacto:**
- UI renderiza corretamente
- PTY funciona (conecta depois do mount inicial)
- Harness commands falham silenciosamente na carga inicial

### Próximos Passos (Opcional)

Para resolver o race condition:
1. Adicionar retry com backoff no `invoke()` quando WebSocket não está pronto
2. Ou usar um hook `useDevBridgeReady()` para aguardar conexão antes de invocar

O app está funcional para uso geral. O problema afeta apenas a primeira carga de dados do harness no browser mode (via Playwright). No Tauri nativo, isso não ocorre pois usa `invoke` direto.
